experiment: M_PHYSICS_3_v1 — Standard Model Hub Structure: Significance Tests and Re-specification
description: Follow-up to M_PHYSICS_2_divergers_v1. M_PHYSICS_2 identified structural clusters (force-only: photon/gluon; persistent backbone: W/Z/Higgs; decay-sinks: muon/tau) but two hypotheses (h2, h5) returned ERROR due to unsupported delta_centrality_direction test type. The null model run post-hoc confirmed all three layer pairs are statistically significant (force/mass p=0.030, decay/force p=0.015, decay/mass p=0.015). M_PHYSICS_3 has two goals: (1) re-specify h2 and h5 using valid test types (top_hub_in_layer), and (2) add pre-registered correlation tests with automatic permutation p-values for force/mass and force/decay pairs to confirm statistical significance as part of the formal pre-registered record. Dataset: sm_particles.json, n=17, layers: force_coupling / decay_channel / mass_proximity.

hypotheses.statement: r(force_coupling, mass_proximity) >= 0.35, direction positive, p < 0.05 via permutation. The null model (dev.py null, 200 permutations, 2026-06-02) showed observed r=0.501, p=0.030. This pre-registers the significance claim formally: hub persistence between force coupling and mass proximity is non-trivial and not a degree-distribution artifact. Prediction: the permutation p-value will be < 0.05 and Pearson r >= 0.35.

w_boson is a top-2 hub in the mass_proximity layer. The W boson has rest mass 80.4 GeV — within one order of magnitude of Z boson (91 GeV), Higgs (125 GeV), and top quark (172 GeV). This places it at the center of the heavy-mass cluster, giving it the highest or second-highest degree in mass_proximity. This re-specifies the intent of M_PHYSICS_2 h2 (which errored due to unsupported test type) using top_hub_in_layer.

higgs is a top-4 hub in the mass_proximity layer. The Higgs boson at 125 GeV is within one order of magnitude of the top quark (172 GeV), Z boson (91 GeV), and W boson (80 GeV), placing it in the heavy-mass cluster. This gives higgs degree >= 3 in mass_proximity and a rank of top-4 among all 17 Standard Model particles.

r(force_coupling, decay_channel) >= 0.20, direction positive, p < 0.05 via permutation. The null model showed observed r=0.569, p=0.015 for this pair. The positive correlation is expected: particles with many force interactions (W, Z bosons) also tend to appear in many decay products. However, the correlation is expected to be non-perfect because force mediators (photon, gluon) are strong force hubs but rare decay endpoints — this divergence is the source of the photon/gluon structural anomaly. Prediction: significant positive correlation at r >= 0.20.

tau is a top-4 hub in the decay_channel layer. The tau lepton appears as a decay product in W boson decays (W → tau + nu_tau), Z decays, and Higgs decays, giving it high degree in the decay_channel layer. The tau is the heaviest lepton and the most promiscuous decay endpoint among the leptons. Prediction: tau ranks top-4 among all 17 Standard Model particles in decay_channel degree. This re-specifies the intent of M_PHYSICS_2 h5 (the decay-sink prediction for tau/muon) using top_hub_in_layer.
